generated_comment: देखो yaar, policy toh fair honi chahiye. Aise har thode time period leave, phir maternity leave, phir childcare flexibility — company project planning kaise karegi? Men ko koi extra leave nahi milta, but appraisal time sabko same hike chahiye, matlab thoda imbalance toh hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biological and caregiving needs as an unfair burden on employers and contrasts them with men’s lack of equivalent leave. It encodes fairness/cost framing and the idea that maternity or menstrual accommodations make women liabilities at work.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
